question: At (54°N, 020°W) the sun rises on November 28th at 09:01 UTC. At (44°N, 020°W) the sun will rise…

earlier since the latter position lies further south.
later since the latter position lies further south.
true: earlier since the latter position lies further south.

inclination of the polar axis with the ecliptic plane.

question: The first law of Kepler states that…
options: the angular speed of the Earth in the orbit around the Sun is constant.
all planets orbit around the Sun at the same speed.
planets move around the Sun in a circular orbit.
planets move in elliptical orbits with the Sun at one of the foci.
true: planets move in elliptical orbits with the Sun at one of the foci.

question: Aphelion is the point of the…
options: Sun’s orbit closest to the Earth.
Earth’s orbit closest to the Sun.
Earth’s orbit furthest away from the Sun.
Sun’s orbit furthest away from the Earth.
true: Earth’s orbit furthest away from the Sun.

question: Kepler's second law states that:
options: The area swept out by the radius vector sun-earth per hour increases with increasing length of the radius vector.
The radius vector sun-earth moves at constant angular speed.
The radius vector sun-earth sweeps out equal areas in equal time.
The length of the radius vector sun-earth is directly proportional to the square root of its angular speed.
true: The radius vector sun-earth sweeps out equal areas in equal time.

question: Which statement regarding the apparent Sun and the mean Sun is correct?
options: The apparent Sun is not important for navigation as there is only a maximum time difference of 4 seconds compared to the mean Sun.
The apparent Sun is the visible Sun, and the mean sun does not exist.
The apparent Sun is a fictitious Sun coupled to UTC, the mean Sun is related to local mean time.
The mean Sun moves along the ecliptic, the apparent Sun moves along the celestial equator.
true: The apparent Sun is the visible Sun, and the mean sun does not exist.

question: The declination of the Sun is defined as the…
options: arc from the celestial horizon to the Sun measured along a vertical line perpendicular on the horizon.
angular distance of the Sun north or south of the celestial equator.
arc of the meridian of the Sun measured from the nearest pole to the Sun.
arc along the celestial sphere from the zenith to the Sun.
true: angular distance of the Sun north or south of the celestial equator.

question: The time interval between sunrise and sunset is dependent on the…
options: date and the longitude of the observer.
latitude and the longitude of the observer.
inclination of the axis of the Earth and the longitude of the observer.
declination of the Sun and the latitude of the observer.
true: declination of the Sun and the latitude of the observer.

question: The main reason for the Earth’s seasons is the…
options: length of the day, as stated by Kepler’s second law.
elliptical orbit of the Earth around the Sun.
distance between the Sun and the Earth.
inclination of the Earth’s polar axis to the plane of the ecliptic.
true: inclination of the Earth’s polar axis to the plane of the ecliptic.

question: In which statement is the Mean Sun best described?
options: The mean sun is a fictitious sun, the orbit of which coincides with that of the apparent sun, but is corrected for mean astronomical and atmospheric refraction.
The mean sun is a fictitious sun, the orbit of which coincides with that of the apparent sun, but is corrected for the mean difference in hour angle.
The mean sun is a fictitious sun travelling along the celestial equator at uniform speed.
The mean sun is a fictitious sun travelling along the ecliptic at uniform speed.
true: The mean sun is a fictitious sun travelling along the celestial equator at uniform speed.

question: Which statement is correct?
options: The planets move around the Sun, like all stars of the Solar System.
The solar system consists of the Sun, planets, and stars.
The Earth is one of the planets that is moving in an elliptical orbit around the Sun.
The Sun moves in an elliptical orbit around the Earth.
true: The Earth is one of the planets that is moving in an elliptical orbit around the Sun.

question: The orbit of the Earth…

is a circle with the Sun at a point next to its centre.
around the Sun is a circle with the Sun at its centre.
around the Sun is an ellipse with the Sun at one of the foci.
true: around the Sun is an ellipse with the Sun at one of the foci.

The apparent solar day is the period between two successive transits of the mean Sun through the same meridian.

true: The apparent solar day is the period between two successive transits of the true Sun through the same meridian.

question: What is the cause of the continuous variation in the length of the apparent solar day throughout the year?
options: The fact that the Earth is closest to the Sun around the 1st of January.

The fact that the Earth is closest to the Sun around the 1st of July.
true: The tilt of the Earth’s axis and the elliptical orbit of the Earth around the Sun.

question: The reason that the solar day lasts longer than the sidereal day is that…
options: both the direction of rotation of the Earth around its axis and its orbital rotation around the Sun are the same.
the orbital speed of the Earth around its axis diminishes slowly.
the orbital track of the Earth around the Sun is an ellipse.
the Sun has an own movement through the universe opposite to the movement of the Earth due to the gravitational forces of celestial bodies.
true: both the direction of rotation of the Earth around its axis and its orbital rotation around the Sun are the same.

question: Which of the following alternatives is correct when you cross the international date line?

Kepler's second law states:
options: The area swept out by the radius vector of each planet, sun to earth, per hour, increasing with increasing length of the radius vector
Each planet revolves so that the radius vector, sun to earth, sweeps out equal area in equal intervals of time
The length of the radius vector, sun to earth, is proportional to the square root of its angular speed
Each planet's radius vector, sun to earth, moves at a constant angular speed
true: Each planet revolves so that the radius vector, sun to earth, sweeps out equal area in equal intervals of time

question: The reason that the solar day lasts longer than the sidereal day is that
options: The orbital speed of the earth around its axis diminishes slowly.
Both the direction of rotation of the Earth around its axis and its orbital rotation around the sun are the same.
The orbital track of the earth around the sun is an ellipse.
The sun has an own movement through the universe opposite to the movement of the earth due to the gravitational forces of celestial bodies.
true: Both the direction of rotation of the Earth around its axis and its orbital rotation around the sun are the same.

question: Which statement about ST is true?

true: Standard time is determined by the government of the appropriate state and does not necessarily follow the borders of 15° wide longitude zones.
